result: Don't ship this as-is — it has serious problems: the AI-generated SQL is executed directly with no validation, so any query (including `DROP TABLE`, data exfiltration, or destructive writes) runs unchecked, making this a SQL-injection-by-design hole over your customer data; there's no authentication or authorization at all, yet you're binding to `0.0.0.0` which exposes it on every network interface, and Flask's dev server (`app.run`) isn't meant for production; you're also leaking raw results with no row limits, no per-user data scoping, and no audit logging. At minimum, run the AI's SQL against a read-only DB connection/user, allowlist to `SELECT` only (parse and reject anything else), add auth + access controls, cap result size, put it behind a real WSGI server, and add logging — I'd push the "tomorrow" deadline.
